loopStyle: delegated to pydantic-ai Agent.run(); harness owns the outer REPL + retries

nodes.label: Read user task (REPL)
nodes.note: The interactive while-True REPL (cli_runner.py:584) reads a task via prompt_toolkit, then dispatches it as an asyncio task to agent.run_with_mcp (cli_runner.py:1025).

nodes.label: run_with_mcp orchestration
nodes.note: Harness-owned wrapper: sanitizes the prompt, fires user_prompt_submit hooks, builds the pydantic-ai agent if needed, prepends the system prompt on turn 1, and installs signal/key-listener cancellation around the run.

nodes.label: pydantic_agent.run() [framework loop]
nodes.note: The ACTUAL model-call/tool-dispatch/feed-results loop lives INSIDE pydantic-ai's Agent.run (pydantic-ai-slim 1.56.0), not in code_puppy. The harness calls it once per turn with message_history, usage_limits, and an event_stream_handler; pydantic-ai internally calls the model, dispatches @agent.tool functions, appends ToolReturnParts, and repeats until the model stops requesting tools.

nodes.label: Dispatch @agent.tool function
nodes.note: Tools are plain Python functions registered on the pydantic-ai Agent via the @agent.tool decorator (register_tools_for_agent, tools/__init__.py:253). pydantic-ai generates JSON schema from the function signature, matches the model's structured tool call by name, validates args, and invokes the function with a RunContext. The protocol is OpenAI/Anthropic function-calling normalized by pydantic-ai — code_puppy never parses tool_use blocks itself.

nodes.label: on_file_permission / shell approval
nodes.note: Approval is per-tool and in-band, not a loop-level gate. Mutating file tools call the on_file_permission callback (callbacks.py:345), which the file_permission_handler plugin routes to get_user_approval (register_callbacks.py:239). Shell commands prompt directly via get_user_approval_async (command_runner.py:1168). YOLO mode (get_yolo_mode) and non-interactive stdin short-circuit the prompt.

nodes.label: Run the tool body
nodes.note: On approval the tool performs its effect (write/replace/delete, shell exec, read, grep) and returns a dict/typed result. pydantic-ai turns that return value into a tool result and feeds it back into the same Agent.run loop.

nodes.label: Return rejection result
nodes.note: On denial the tool returns a rejection dict (_create_rejection_response) — optionally carrying the user's feedback text (USER REJECTED: ...) — instead of raising. pydantic-ai feeds that string back to the model as the tool result, so the turn continues with the effect withheld.

nodes.label: Stream / render assistant text
nodes.note: When streaming is enabled, the event_stream_handler consumes pydantic-ai PartStart/PartDelta/PartEnd events and renders text/thinking/tool activity live; a StreamingTextDetector falls back to a one-shot render if nothing streamed (_runtime.py:437).

nodes.label: Drain steers / hook retries
nodes.note: After Agent.run returns, a harness-level while-True drains queued 'steer' injections and plugin-requested hook retries, issuing follow-up Agent.run calls (capped) before the turn is considered done. This is code_puppy's own outer loop around the framework's inner loop.

nodes.label: Turn complete
nodes.note: run_with_mcp awaits the agent task, extracts the response text, prunes interrupted tool calls from history, tears down the key listener/signal handler, and returns control to the REPL for the next user task.

scenarios.title: Model answers with no tools
scenarios.note: pydantic-ai's Agent.run returns after a single model call with no tool calls; the harness renders the streamed text and, with no steers/retries queued, completes the turn.

scenarios.title: User asks to edit a file
scenarios.note: The model emits a function call for a file-edit tool; pydantic-ai dispatches it, the tool calls on_file_permission, the user approves, the write runs, the result feeds back into the same Agent.run, and the model finishes.

scenarios.title: User denies a file edit
scenarios.note: Denial is handled inside the tool: it returns a rejection result (optionally with feedback) rather than raising. pydantic-ai feeds that string back to the model as the tool result, so the model continues the turn with the edit withheld. There is no loop-level deny path — it is just an ordinary tool return.

scenarios.title: Model issues several tool calls
scenarios.note: pydantic-ai dispatches multiple tool calls within one Agent.run; each mutating tool independently invokes its own approval prompt (serialized by the module-level approval locks in tools/common.py), then all tool results feed back to the model.
